title: Change hint bar separator color to 242
description:
Per Inbox/Updates: The │ separator in the hint bar should be more gray (color 242).

Current state in hint_bar.rs Widget::render:
```rust
buf[(quit_start_x, last_line_y)]
    .set_char('│')
    .set_fg(colors::WHITE);
```

Changes needed:
- Add SEPARATOR_GREY constant as Color::Indexed(242) in colors.rs
- Update the separator rendering to use SEPARATOR_GREY instead of WHITE

title: tab should focus on the selected session's terminal pane just like enter does.

title: Review: UI Overhaul Phase 1 - State Machine and Focus Management
description:
Final review for UI overhaul Phase 1:
- Verify all implementation issues are closed
- Run all tests (unit, integration, E2E)
- Verify no tests are skipped
- Check for any warnings or clippy issues
- Verify all spec requirements from objectives.md are met:
  * State machine with focus and mode management
  * Hint bar with dynamic keybindings and quit path
  * Sidebar session list with selection and scrolling
  * All keybindings work as specified
  * Spec 256-colors applied
  * Confirmation prompts for delete and quit
  * Create and rename modes with text input
  * Welcome state for first-time startup
- Visual inspection in Apple Terminal
- Re-install sb binary for user testing

title: Implement terminal state serialization for session restore
description:
Add terminal state serialization to the daemon for restoring sessions on reconnect:
1. Daemon maintains vt100 parser state (already have vt100 crate)
2. All PTY output fed to vt100 parser for state tracking
3. On client reconnect, serialize terminal state from vt100 screen buffer
4. Send serialized state to client to restore screen contents
5. Include cursor position, colors, and text attributes in serialization

Reference: zmx serializeTerminalState in references/session-persistence/zmx/src/main.zig line 1505
Reference: shpool session_restore in references/session-persistence/shpool/libshpool/src/session_restore/mod.rs

title: Add workspace E2E tests (all workspace spec requirements)
description:
Write the mandatory E2E tests for workspace functionality per the spec.

DONE means (all these E2E tests pass):
- test_create_workspace: create a new workspace via ctrl+w -> n, verify it appears in overlay
- test_switch_workspace: switch between workspaces, verify sessions are isolated (session in workspace A not visible when in workspace B)
- test_rename_workspace: rename a workspace via ctrl+w -> r, verify new name in sidebar and overlay
- test_delete_workspace: delete a workspace via ctrl+w -> d -> y, verify it's gone and its sessions are gone
- test_move_session_between_workspaces: move a session from one workspace to another via m, verify isolation
- test_workspace_state_restored: switch away from workspace, come back, verify last selected session and focus are restored
- test_workspace_auto_create_default: on fresh start, 'Default' workspace is auto-created
- test_delete_last_workspace_creates_default: delete last workspace, verify new Default is auto-created
- test_workspace_persists_across_restart: create workspace with sessions, restart, verify workspace and sessions survive

These tests verify the spec requirements on lines 21-22 and 156-192.

Resources:
- tests/e2e/mod.rs has SbSession helper patterns
- SbSession needs send_ctrl_w() helper method
- These tests should follow the same isolation patterns as existing E2E tests (cleanup_test_sessions, SbSession::new, etc.)

- Handle welcome state (no sessions) properly
- Integration tests with daemon

Reference: src/daemon.rs for existing protocol

title: Research: Fix Enter crash - Broken pipe error
description:
CRITICAL BUG: When pressing enter in sidebar to select a session, TUI crashes with:
```
Error:
  0: Broken pipe (os error 32)

Location:
  src/main.rs:284

Backtrace omitted. Run with RUST_BACKTRACE=1 environment variable to display it.
```

Research required:
- Understand the exact cause of the broken pipe
- Look at what happens when SwitchSession event is triggered
- Check daemon communication flow on session selection
- Review send_daemon_message and stream handling

This is blocking core functionality - selecting and switching between sessions doesn't work.

title: Fix misleading placeholder text about Ctrl+Q quitting
description: The terminal placeholder text (main.rs line 771) says 'press Ctrl+Q or Ctrl+B to quit' but neither key directly quits. Ctrl+B focuses the sidebar, and then you need to press 'q' to get the quit confirmation. The text should say something like 'press Ctrl+B then q to quit' or just refer to the hint bar for keybindings.

title: Sometimes the session name text color turns grey instead of white in the apple terminal when I move between terminal sessions. No idea why.
Move terminal padding inside border
description:
Per Inbox/Updates: The terminal padding is currently being applied outside of the border it should be inside the border between the border and the terminal content.

Current state in main.rs:
```rust
let terminal_area = pad_rect_horizontal(horizontal_chunks[1], TERMINAL_H_PADDING);
render_terminal_emulator_with_state(frame, terminal_area, &app.term_emulator, &app.app_state);
```

The padding is applied to the Rect before rendering the block with borders, so the borders are drawn on the padded rect and the padding is outside.

Changes needed:
- Render the terminal block on the full horizontal_chunks[1] area
- Apply padding to inner_area after getting it from block.inner()
- This ensures padding is between border and content

4. Verify vi is still open with the same content
5. Verify can save and exit vi normally

This tests the core session persistence functionality required by objectives.

title: Move PTY ownership from TUI to daemon
description:
Refactor the TUI to become a client that connects to the session daemon instead of directly owning the PTY:
1. Move PTY creation from main.rs to daemon
2. Daemon spawns shell and owns PTY read/write
3. TUI connects to daemon via Unix socket
4. Daemon forwards PTY output to all connected clients
5. TUI sends keyboard input to daemon, daemon forwards to PTY

Add E2E test for Claude agent session
description:
Per Inbox/Updates: There needs to be an E2E test that tests launching 'claude'. Sometimes depending on how we mess with the session logic it shows the error about nested Claude Code sessions.

This is a CRITICAL test because:
- One of the main use cases for this TUI is to run agents in terminal sessions
- 'claude' is the main way users do that right now
- The nested session error appears intermittently depending on build changes

The E2E test should:
1. Create a new agent session (which runs 'claude')
2. Verify the session starts without the 'nested sessions' error
3. Verify the terminal shows claude's output (not the error message)

Error to watch for:
```
Error: Claude Code cannot be launched inside another Claude Code session. Nested sessions share runtime resources and wil crash all active sessions. To bypass this check, unset the CLAUDECODE environment variable.
```

If this error appears, the test should fail and we need to investigate why CLAUDECODE env var is being passed to terminal sessions.
close_reason: Fixed the CLAUDECODE environment variable issue that caused the 'nested Claude Code session' error. Added cmd.env_remove in pty.rs, unit test, and E2E test test_agent_session_no_nested_error. All 379 tests pass.

notes: Detect crossterm Event::Resize. Update PTY size with master.resize(PtySize). Update vt100 parser dimensions with parser.set_size(). Recalculate layout constraints. Reference: wezterm resize handling.

title: Add dirty tracking for incremental rendering
description:
Every frame completely reconstructs all styled spans from scratch. Add infrastructure to track which rows/cells changed and skip re-rendering unchanged content.

This requires:
- Tracking previous frame state
- Comparing current vs previous
- Only rebuilding changed portions
- Touches multiple files

Location: src/terminal.rs:166-258 (entire render function)

Estimate: 8 story points

Note: This is a larger refactor that should come after the quick wins are implemented.
notes: After investigation: This requires significant refactoring. The render() method takes &self, making direct caching difficult. Options explored: 1) Use RefCell for interior mutability, 2) Change signature to &mut self, 3) Move caching to the caller level. The vt100 Screen implements Clone but cloning each frame is expensive. Recommend breaking into sub-tasks: (a) Add dirty flag to Terminal, (b) Change render() to &mut self, (c) Add cached Line storage, (d) Integrate with main loop to skip render when not dirty.

title: Add vt100 parser for terminal emulation
notes: Add vt100 dependency. Create Parser with terminal dimensions. Feed PTY output to parser. Implement rendering from vt100::Screen to ratatui Paragraph widget with proper color mapping. Reference: vt100 examples, terminal-emulation RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md.

title: Research: Fully Async Message Queue Architecture
description: Research converting all daemon operations to use the async MessageReader pattern with a pending operations queue.
notes:
## Goal
Understand what it would take to make all operations async-first, eliminating the problematic synchronous decode_message() calls.

## Areas to Explore
1. Current synchronous operations in send_daemon_message() - what calls it?
2. How to implement a pending operations queue
3. How to match responses to requests (request IDs?)
4. Existing async patterns in the references/ folder

## Key Questions
- What operations currently require synchronous responses?
- Can we make CreateSession, DeleteSession, Rename etc all async?
- How do we handle error responses for specific operations?
- Is this overkill for just the Preview feature?
close_reason: Completed research. Created detailed analysis at references/fully_async_spike.md. Key finding: Fully async architecture with request_id tracking is architecturally superior but overkill for just Preview. Recommends Hybrid Drain-Before-Sync (sidebar_tui-dhj) for immediate implementation.

title: Research: Hybrid Message Handling with Synchronization
description: Research how to properly synchronize the MessageReader buffered approach with synchronous decode_message() calls for the live preview feature.
notes:
## Research Complete

### Key Finding: **Hybrid approach IS viable** with drain-before-sync pattern

### Problem Root Cause
The conflict occurs because:
1. MessageReader buffers data in the main async loop
2. send_daemon_message uses decode_message which calls read_exact() directly on socket
3. When Preview messages are sent async, their responses can be intercepted by sync ops OR orphaned in MessageReader's buffer

### Solution: Drain Before Sync
Before any synchronous operation (CreateSession, DeleteSession, etc.):
1. Process all complete messages from MessageReader buffer (try_parse_buffered)
2. Read any pending messages from socket with short timeout
3. Clear any partial data from buffer

### Implementation Effort
- About 30 lines of code for drain function
- 5-6 call sites to update in main.rs
- Helper methods already exist (clear, has_buffered_data, try_parse_buffered)

### Risk Assessment
- Low: Data loss during clear() is acceptable since sync ops immediately restore state
- Medium: Need comprehensive E2E tests for rapid navigation scenarios

### Detailed Analysis
See: references/hybrid_sync_spike.md

### Recommendation
This approach works but adds complexity. Consider comparing with other research issues (separate sockets, fully async) before choosing.

title: Eliminate per-character String allocation in render
description:
The render function creates a new String for every cell on every frame (~1,920 allocations per render at 60 FPS).

Fix options:
- Use Cow<str> for cell contents
- Pre-allocate string buffers
- Use static strings for common cases (single space)

Locations:
- src/terminal.rs:228-250 (non-scrolled path)
- src/terminal.rs:175-226 (scrolled path)
- src/terminal.rs:194 (history clone)

Estimate: 3 story points

- Use serde_json for persistence same as sessions

title: Research: Separate Socket Channels for Stream vs Request-Response
description: Research using two separate Unix socket connections: one dedicated to streaming Output, one for request-response operations like Preview.
notes:
## Research Complete

Analyzed the separate socket channels approach. Key findings:

1. **Option A (True Dual Sockets)**: High complexity - requires handshake protocol, client ID tracking, socket registry, coordination
2. **Option B (Internal Channels)**: Medium complexity - background reader thread + mpsc channels, no daemon changes

Zellij uses internal channels (crossbeam mpsc) rather than multiple sockets for Unix IPC. Their web client does use dual WebSocket channels but that's a different protocol.

**Recommendation**: Option B (Internal Channels) is cleaner than dual sockets but still more complex than the hybrid drain-before-sync approach from sidebar_tui-dhj. For just the Preview feature, drain-before-sync may be sufficient.

Full analysis at: references/separate_sockets_spike.md

title: Add E2E test: welcome text dynamic keybinding update - when no sessions exist and user switches focus from sidebar to terminal pane, the welcome text should show mod+n instead of n
E2E test: character restrictions enforced in session name drafting

When I move the selected session up and down the terminal view should show the selected session's terminal content as I move through the list, not just after I hit enter to select it. Requires architectural refactor of message handling. We should plan and do this.
notes:
## Overview
This is the planning issue for implementing live terminal preview as you navigate sessions in the sidebar (instead of only showing the terminal after pressing Enter to select).

## Background
A previous attempt (sidebar_tui-xjh) was blocked due to architectural issues. The problem is that the codebase uses two incompatible message handling approaches:
1. **Synchronous** (send_daemon_message): Uses blocking decode_message() with read_exact()
2. **Asynchronous** (MessageReader): Non-blocking buffered reads in the main event loop

When Preview messages are sent during the main loop, the async MessageReader's buffer can conflict with the synchronous decode_message() calls, corrupting the message stream.

## Existing Infrastructure (Implemented but Disabled)
- ClientMessage::Preview and DaemonResponse::Previewed in daemon.rs
- Preview handler in daemon (reads terminal state without attaching)
- EventResult::PreviewSession in state.rs
- Handler code in main.rs event loop (lines 396-402)
- MessageReader helper methods: clear(), has_buffered_data(), try_parse_buffered()

## Research Phase
Created 3 research issues to explore architectural options:
1. sidebar_tui-dhj: Hybrid approach with MessageReader synchronization
2. sidebar_tui-hp0: Separate socket channels for stream vs request-response
3. sidebar_tui-dcr: Fully async message queue architecture

After research is complete, we'll analyze findings and create implementation issues.
## Implementation Plan

Based on research, proceeding with **Hybrid Drain-Before-Sync** approach.

### Why This Approach?
- Minimal code: ~30 lines for drain function + 5-6 call sites
- No protocol changes required
- Helper methods already exist (clear, has_buffered_data, try_parse_buffered)
- Low risk since sync ops immediately restore state anyway

### Implementation Steps
1. Add drain_async_messages() helper function in main.rs
2. Update each sync operation site to call drain before send_daemon_message
3. Enable the PreviewSession event handler in input_handler.rs
4. Add E2E tests for preview + rapid navigation scenarios
close_reason: Planning complete. Created 5 implementation issues: l82.1 (drain helper), l82.2 (update call sites), l82.3 (enable preview), l82.4 (E2E tests), l82.5 (review). Proceeding with Hybrid Drain-Before-Sync approach.

title: Implement drain_async_messages() helper function
description: Add a drain_async_messages() function in main.rs that processes all pending async messages before sync operations.
notes:
## Implementation

Create function that:
1. Processes all complete messages from MessageReader buffer via try_parse_buffered()
2. Reads any pending messages from socket with short timeout (50ms)
3. Handles Preview and Output responses appropriately
4. Clears any partial data from buffer

Reference: references/hybrid_sync_spike.md lines 67-104

title: Update sync operations to drain before sending
description: Update all send_daemon_message() call sites to first call drain_async_messages().
notes:
## Call Sites to Update (main.rs)

1. CreateSession handler (~line 436) - ClientMessage::Attach
2. DeleteSession handler (~line 477) - ClientMessage::Kill
3. DeleteSession post-switch (~line 487) - ClientMessage::Attach
4. RenameSession handler (~line 518) - ClientMessage::Rename
5. SwitchSession handler (~line 547-550) - ClientMessage::Detach + Attach
6. Initial List/Attach on startup (~lines 310, 332) - may not need drain since no async yet

After each sync operation, reset the stream timeout back to 10ms for async mode.

Reference the disabled code location and verify integration with drain_async_messages().

title: Add E2E tests for live preview scenarios
description: Add comprehensive E2E tests for the live preview feature covering rapid navigation and edge cases.
notes:
## Test Scenarios

1. **Basic Preview Test**: Navigate sidebar up/down, verify terminal shows previewed session content
2. **Rapid Navigation Test**: Rapidly press down/up keys, verify no crashes/corruption
3. **Preview + Select Test**: Navigate to preview a session, then press Enter to select, verify correct session attached
4. **Preview + Create Test**: Preview a session, then create new session (Ctrl+N), verify no message interleaving
5. **Preview + Delete Test**: Preview a session, then delete different session, verify state consistency
6. **Preview + Switch Test**: Preview session A, select session B, verify correct attachment

Reference: references/hybrid_sync_spike.md edge cases section

title: Create session daemon module with Unix socket server

Create src/daemon.rs module that implements a session daemon:
1. Unix socket server per session (one daemon per terminal session)
2. Socket file location in XDG_RUNTIME_DIR or /tmp/sidebar-tui-{uid}/
3. Daemon process that forks and detaches from TUI
4. Session naming for identifying/reconnecting to sessions
5. Message protocol for communication between TUI client and daemon
6. Signal handling for graceful shutdown (SIGTERM, SIGHUP)

title: Add session persistence across reboots
description:
Persist session metadata to disk for survival across system reboots:
1. Save session metadata (name, command, cwd) to disk on creation
2. Metadata location in XDG_DATA_HOME or ~/.local/share/sidebar-tui/
3. On TUI startup, check for persisted sessions and offer to restore
4. Optionally auto-restore sessions on startup
5. Clean up stale session files for sessions that no longer exist

Note: Per objectives, user wants to pick up vi editing where they left off. Full process state cannot survive reboot, but command can be re-run with warning like Zellij does.

title: Use VecDeque for terminal history
description:
Replace Vec with VecDeque for history storage.

Current code uses history.remove(0) which is O(n) - shifts all 10,000 elements on every removal.

Fix: Change to VecDeque and use pop_front() which is O(1).

  - KeyCode::Char('j') → select_next() (Down)

title: Render batching: drain socket before rendering
description:
Core architectural fix: Change main loop to read all available data from socket before rendering once per frame.

Currently each network packet triggers: capture → parse → render → poll
Should be: drain all packets → parse all → render once → poll

This is the root cause multiplier - every other inefficiency happens N times per paste instead of once.

Location: src/main.rs:568-603

Estimate: 3 story points

Investigate approaches for persisting terminal sessions across TUI restarts and even reboots:
1. tmux integration - running shell sessions inside tmux and reattaching
2. screen integration - similar to tmux
3. CRIU (Checkpoint/Restore) - Linux-specific kernel feature

5. What limitations exist for each approach

Goal: Determine the best approach for making vi/vim editing sessions survive TUI close and reopen, even across reboots. Clone relevant docs/code to references/ folder.

title: Reduce unnecessary screen captures
description:
Stop capturing screen to history on every process() call.

Currently capture_screen_to_history() is called before processing each data chunk. When pasting large text, dozens of packets arrive rapidly, each triggering a full screen scan of 1,920+ cells.

Only capture when actually needed:
- Before scrolling
- On resize events
- When explicitly requested

Location: src/terminal.rs:87-98

Estimate: 2 story points

- Overlay shows: 'Workspaces' title in purple, list of all workspaces, active workspace marked with '*', selected workspace highlighted with dark grey bg (color 238)
- Truncation indicators ('...') appear at top/bottom if list overflows
- Navigation: up/k, down/j move selection
- enter: switch to selected workspace, close overlay, restore workspace's last saved state
- n: inline draft new workspace (same character restrictions as session names)
- r: inline rename selected workspace
- d: show important (dark red bg, color 88) confirmation: 'Delete workspace and ALL its sessions permanently?' with y/n
- esc: close overlay without switching
- q: show quit confirmation same as sidebar
- Hint bar shows correct keybindings for overlay
- Hint bar quit path shows 'q Quit' when overlay is open

notes:
## Research Complete

Cloned gitui and bottom projects to references/state-management/ and analyzed state management patterns from:
- Ratatui examples (demo2, user-input, popup)
- Zellij InputMode enum
- gitui Component trait and Focus management
- bottom ProcessKillDialog state machine

Created comprehensive RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md with recommended patterns:
1. Primary Focus enum (Sidebar vs Terminal)
2. AppMode enum with state variants (Normal, CreateMode, Drafting, Renaming, Confirming)
3. Modal state handling takes precedence over focus-based handling
4. EventResult enum for event consumption/bubbling
5. Previous focus tracking for 'jump back' on Esc

Key references:
- references/state-management/gitui/ - Component trait with focused()/focus() methods, EventState enum
- references/state-management/bottom/ - ProcessKillDialog state machine, AppSearchState for text input
- references/state-management/RESEARCH_SUMMARY.md - Full documentation with code examples

Show terminal content on selection change
description:
Per Inbox/Updates: When I move the selected session up and down the terminal view should show the selected session's terminal content as I move through the list, not just after I hit enter to select it.

Current behavior:
- Terminal pane only updates when Enter is pressed to select a session
- Moving selection with arrow keys only highlights in sidebar

Required behavior:
- As user navigates with Up/Down arrows, the terminal pane should preview the selected session's content
- This is a UX improvement for browsing sessions

Implementation considerations:
- This requires requesting terminal content from daemon for the highlighted session
- May need a new daemon message type for 'preview' vs 'attach'
- Consider caching recent terminal states for quick preview
- Need to handle the case where preview session differs from active session
notes: BLOCKED: Live preview feature implemented but disabled due to message flow issues. The async Preview message interferes with the buffered MessageReader, causing connection errors. The code is in place but disabled with a TODO comment in input_handler.rs. Needs refactoring to either: (1) Use fully synchronous operations for Preview, or (2) Refactor all operations to be fully asynchronous through MessageReader. Created infrastructure: PreviewSession EventResult variant, ClientMessage::Preview, DaemonResponse::Previewed, handler code in main.rs and daemon.rs.
close_reason: Superseded by sidebar_tui-l82 series which implemented the drain-before-sync approach. Live preview is now fully functional - navigating the sidebar with arrow keys or j/k immediately shows the selected session's terminal content.
